message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="STOP - concurrent file edits violating rules">
**STOP — COMMIT DISCIPLINE VIOLATION**

Multiple files are being modified concurrently. Carson is the SOLE driver for Task #3. I see changes to register.html, register.rs, and main.css that are NOT part of Task #3.

**EVERYONE except Carson: STOP modifying files immediately.** Do not touch any files until Carson commits, pushes, and confirms CI green.

If you have made changes, DO NOT save them. Back away from the files now.

Carson is the only authorized driver. One driver at a time. This is non-negotiable.
</teammate-message>

content.text: I have not modified any files. My working tree should be clean. Let me verify.

input.description: Verify my working tree is clean

content.text: There are modified files in the working tree, but I did NOT make any of these changes. These appear to be from other teammates editing concurrently. I have not touched any files since Task #1 was committed.

input.content:
Confirmed: I (Scott) have NOT modified any files. My last file edit was during the early Task #1 review before Luca was confirmed as driver, and that was already committed in 9cdc020.

However, the shared working tree has dirty state from multiple teammates. The modified files include `src/routes/todos.rs`, `static/css/main.css`, `templates/base.html`, `templates/register.html`, `templates/todos.html`, `tests/api/register.rs`, `tests/api/todos.rs`, plus new untracked files (`static/js/`, `templates/todo_item.html`).

Some of these look like Carson's Task #3 work (base.html for HTMX script, todos.rs for HX-Request handling, todo_item.html fragment template). Others (register.html, register.rs) do not belong to Task #3.

I am hands-off. Not touching any files. Waiting for resolution.
input.summary: Confirmed I made no changes, reporting dirty state

routing.summary: Confirmed I made no changes, reporting dirty state
